---
title: "Thematic classification of Right-to-Health recommendations in the UPR"
subtitle: "Keyword-based, reproducible classification of UHRI/UPR recommendations"
author: "Anshu Uppal"
date: "2026-06-16"
date-modified: last-modified # Dynamic modified date
format:
        html:
                code-fold: true
                toc: true
                toc-location: left  # Place TOC on the left side
                toc-depth: 5        # Include headers up to level 5 (#####)
                toc-title: "On this page" # Custom title for the TOC
                smooth-scroll: true # Enable smooth scrolling
                embed-resources: true # Make the html file self contained
                grid:
                  body-width: 1100px
                  margin-width: 100px
reference-location: margin
citation-location: margin
fig-format: svg
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    dev: svglite
    fig.path: output/figures/
---

## Purpose

This document identifies **health-related** recommendations issued under the
Universal Periodic Review (UPR) and assigns each one to one or more **themes** and
**sub-themes**, following the *Codebook of operational definitions for thematic
classification of health-related UPR recommendations*
(`data/Codebook of operational definitions for thematic classification of health.docx`).

Design principles:

- **Keyword-based and fully reproducible.** Classification depends only on regular-expression
matches against the recommendation text (`text_2`). No embeddings, no LLM, no randomness —
re-running the code on the same input always yields the same output.
- **Definitions are separated from the matching engine.** Every (sub-)theme is expressed as a
small *spec* (`include` / `include_all` / `exclude`) in one auditable registry, so a reviewer
can read each category's rule next to its codebook definition.
- **Multi-label.** A recommendation can belong to several themes at once.
- **Hierarchy-aware.** If a recommendation matches a sub-theme it is also tagged into its
parent theme, and any tagged theme implies the overall *Health-related* umbrella.

> **Note on this version.** The keyword definitions below are ported **verbatim** from the first
> attempt (`02_prep_UHRI_recommendation_definitions.qmd`) so that results remain directly
> comparable. Where the codebook specifies a refinement that the first attempt does not yet
> implement, it is left as a clearly-marked comment (`# CODEBOOK REFINEMENT:`) rather than
> silently changing the matching behaviour.

## Setup

```{r}
#| label: setup
pacman::p_load(
  here,
  tidyverse,
  janitor,
  DT
)
```

### Load the pre-processed UPR recommendations

Download and pre-processing of the raw UHRI export live in
`02_prep_UHRI_recommendation_definitions.qmd`, which writes `data/UHRI_UPR.rds`.
This file consumes that output and focuses solely on classification.

```{r}
#| label: load-data
uhri_upr <- readRDS(here("data", "UHRI_UPR.rds"))

# Display labels for each theme variable
source(here("code", "theme_labels.R"))

# Classification is performed on the cleaned recommendation text (`text_2`).
# Lower-case it once; treat missing text as empty so every str_detect() returns FALSE
# (mirrors the first attempt, where an NA condition falls through to "Other").
txt     <- tolower(uhri_upr$text_2)
txt[is.na(txt)] <- ""
# A small number of codebook rules are matched against the original (non-lowercased)
# text in the first attempt; keep a raw copy to reproduce that behaviour exactly.
txt_raw <- uhri_upr$text_2
txt_raw[is.na(txt_raw)] <- ""
```

For the present analysis, the dataset was downloaded on
`r unique(uhri_upr$date_accessed)`. There are
`r format(nrow(uhri_upr), big.mark = ",")` recommendations to classify.

## The matching engine

The engine is deliberately tiny. A **rule** fires when *all* of its `all` patterns match and
*none* of its `none` patterns match. A **spec** fires when *any* of its rules fire and the
global `exclude` pattern does not match. Each pattern is an ordinary regex (an `|`-separated
list of keywords), exactly as in the first attempt.

```{r}
#| label: engine

# A single rule: AND across `all`, AND-NOT across `none`.
match_rule <- function(txt, rule) {
  hit <- rep(TRUE, length(txt))
  for (p in rule$all)  hit <- hit & str_detect(txt, p)
  for (p in rule$none) hit <- hit & !str_detect(txt, p)
  hit
}

# A spec: OR across rules, minus a global exclusion.
match_spec <- function(txt, spec) {
  fired <- rep(FALSE, length(txt))
  for (r in spec$rules) fired <- fired | match_rule(txt, r)
  if (!is.null(spec$exclude)) fired <- fired & !str_detect(txt, spec$exclude)
  fired
}

# Convenience constructors so the registry reads cleanly.
rule <- function(..., none = NULL) list(all = c(...), none = none)
spec <- function(..., exclude = NULL) list(rules = list(...), exclude = exclude)

# QA helper: given a combined keyword pattern, return the keywords that actually matched
# in each text (used only for the spot-check tables, not for classification).
matched_terms <- function(txt, pattern) {
  if (is.null(pattern) || !nzchar(pattern)) return(rep(NA_character_, length(txt)))
  vapply(
    str_extract_all(txt, pattern),
    function(m) if (length(m)) paste(unique(m), collapse = "; ") else NA_character_,
    character(1)
  )
}
```

## Keyword definitions

Keywords are grouped by codebook category. Each block reproduces the relevant codebook
definition (as a quote) followed by the keyword vectors ported from the first attempt.

### Right to health (umbrella)

Broad key-terms used to capture any health-related recommendation not already caught by a
specific theme.

```{r}
#| label: kw-health
health_keywords <- "health|sanitation|sanitary|safe water|clean water|right to water|disease|sick|infectio|nutrition|tuberculosis|malaria|with hiv| hiv |hiv/aids|hiv-aids|hiv and aids|hiv-positive| sti | tb |hospital|clinic|vaccin|immunisation|immunization|virus|viral|medic|doctor|nurse|nursing|maternal|contracep|abortion|reproductive|pregnan|prenatal|postnatal|neonatal|breastfe|obstetric|fertility|medic|diabetes|cancer|blood|hypertension|respiratory|prophyla|violence against women|violence against children|domestic violence|family violence|violence in the family|gender-based violence|sexual violence|marital violence|gender violence| rape|virginity|malnutrition|obesity|sexual abuse|mortality|leprosy|tobacco|smoking|child marriage| forced marriage|early marriage|infanticide|mutilation|honor crime|honor killing|honour crime|honour killing|breast ironing|harmful practices|harmful traditional practices|epidem|contracept|sex education|suicide|femicide|polygam|covid|pandemic|corona|palliative|lead poisoning|diarrhea|diarrhoea"

# REFINEMENT (FP): exclude violence-context uses of "blood" (the bare "blood" keyword above
# otherwise tags "bloodshed", "bloody repression", etc. as health-related).
NOT_health_keywords <- "blood feud|bloodshed|bloody|cold blood|spilling of blood|shed blood"
```

### Health systems and services

> This category captures recommendations addressing the broader health system and the
> population's general access to health services... Recommendations relating to immunization
> programs, vaccination campaigns, and the general availability of medicines and pharmaceutical
> products are also included here.
>
> Decision rule: ...When a recommendation simultaneously calls for general system-level
> improvements alongside actions on a specific issue or population, it is dual-classified under
> Health systems and services and the relevant sub-category.

```{r}
#| label: kw-hss
hss_keywords <- "health worker|healthcare worker|health workforce|health-care worker|uhc|universal health|health system|healthcare system|health-care system|health care system|health service|healthcare service|health-care service|health care service| oral health|health and education|health coverage|public health|free health|access to health|quality health|strenghthen health|right to health|rights to health|guarantee health|right to a health|guarantee the rights of life, education and health|guarantee the right of health|guarantee the rights to education and health|rights of the Palestinian people, inter alia, to housing, education, health|improve its health - care service|guaranteeing the provision of health|guarantee health care and education|guarantee health assistance to all|achieving health-related goals|highest level of health possible|strengthen its policies in the fields of education and health|programmes aimed at guaranteeing the rights to education, health|programmes to guarantee the rights to education, health|guarantee essential health care for all citizens|digital health|emergency care|critical care|healthy environment|health promotion polic|large scale public health|effective poverty reduction and health strategies|improve the level of education and health|free basic healthcare|national census on health care|abuja declaration|national health insurance"
hss_other_keywords <- "data"   # only counted when the recommendation is already health-related

hss_keywords_comb1a <- "health"
hss_keywords_comb1b <- "capacity-building|coverage|infrastructure|human resources|universal access|affordab|facilities| access | public |budget|social and economic right|human right"

hss_keywords_comb2a <- "health"
hss_keywords_comb2b <- "sector|system|program"
hss_keywords_comb2c <- "financ"

# REFINEMENT (FN): unambiguous SYSTEM-/POPULATION-level language. These signal the broader health
# system or the population's general access, so they are appropriate to tag (and to dual-classify
# alongside a specific theme, per the codebook). Deliberately avoids bare "medical care/service",
# which is too generic to imply a system-level recommendation on its own.
hss_system_keywords <- "health sector|health-care system|health care system|healthcare system|health system|health financ|health budget|health insurance|health workforce|health-care workforce|health policy|health polic|health strateg|health plan|national health|health governance|health infrastructure|universal health| uhc |right to health|rights to health|highest attainable standard of health|health of the population|health of its population|health of the people|state of health of the|primary health care|primary healthcare|primary health-care|health-care deliver|healthcare deliver|health care deliver|health-care reform|healthcare reform|public health system|invest in the health|investment in the health|expenditure on health|spending on health|resources to the health sector|resources for the health"

# Looser general-access language used ONLY to rescue still-residual (other_health_related) recs
# into Health systems - never applied to recs already in a specific theme, so incidental "medical
# care" mentions cannot over-tag specific recommendations.
hss_residual_access <- "medical care|medical treatment|medical service|medical attention|medical assistance|access to health|access to medical|access to quality health|provision of health|quality medical|quality health|quality of health|health of the population|level of health|standard of health|develop the health|developing the health|expenditure on health|invest in health|investment in health|allocate.*health sector|resources.*health sector|right to health|health care|health-care|healthcare|health centre|health center|health facilit|health personnel|mobile clinic|primary care"
```

### Health security, emergencies, and disaster relief

> This category covers recommendations addressing health in the context of humanitarian
> emergencies, disasters, and other situations posing significant threats to population health...
>
> Decision rule: Recommendations addressing strictly non-health dimensions of emergencies
> (e.g. the economic consequences of a pandemic) are excluded from this category.

```{r}
#| label: kw-emergencies
emergencies_keywords <- "covid|pandemic|epidemic|drought|humanitarian emergency|health emergency"

emergencies_keywords_comb1a <- "disaster"
emergencies_keywords_comb1b <- "health|life|wellbeing|well-being"

emergencies_keywords_comb2a <- "earthquake|flood|drought|tsunami|hurricane"
emergencies_keywords_comb2b <- "relief"
```

### Non-communicable diseases

> This category encompasses recommendations addressing non-communicable diseases (NCDs),
> including cardiovascular disease, cancer, chronic respiratory conditions..., diabetes, and
> substance use disorders involving alcohol and drugs.

```{r}
#| label: kw-ncd
ncd_keywords <- "non-communicable|cardiovascular|stroke|cancer|neoplasm|chronic diseas|hypertension|diabet|alcohol|tobacco|smoking|kidney| liver |air pollution|drug dependence|polluted air|leukaemia|leukemia|dementia|addictive problems"

ncd_keywords_comb1a <- " drug"
# REFINEMENT (FP): the bare " use " token matched non-drug uses ("use of the death penalty",
# "use of force") and combined with " drug" mis-tagged death-penalty-for-drug-offences recs as
# NCD. Restrict to genuine drug-use/consumption language.
ncd_keywords_comb1b <- "addict|abuse|drug use|use of drug|use of illicit|use of illegal|use of narcotic|drug consumption|consumption of drug|misuse|harm"

ncd_keywords_comb2a <- " air "
ncd_keywords_comb2b <- "pollution| quality"
```

### Communicable diseases

> This category includes recommendations addressing infectious and zoonotic diseases...
>
> Decision rule: Recommendations relating to COVID-19 are excluded from this category and
> instead classified under Health security, emergencies, and disaster relief. Recommendations
> related to HIV, STIs, tuberculosis and malaria and other Neglected Tropical Diseases are
> excluded from this category and instead classified under "HIV/AIDS and STIs" and
> "TB, Malaria, and NTDs".

```{r}
#| label: kw-communicable
communicable_keywords <- " communicable|infectious|zoonotic|pathogen|parasit|fungal|ebola|measles|yellow fever|influenza|polio|norovirus|vector|water-borne|trachoma| one health| one-health|zika|mpox"

communicable_keywords_comb1a <- " virus|^virus"
# Combinations with the following are exempt (these fall purely under HIV/AIDS):
communicable_keywords_comb1b <- "with hiv| hiv |hiv/aids|hiv-aids|hiv and aids|hiv-positive| hiv,| hiv-"

# CODEBOOK REFINEMENT: the codebook also excludes COVID-19, TB/malaria and NTDs from the
# stand-alone "Communicable diseases" bucket. The current keyword set does not contain
# covid/tb/malaria terms, so they do not match here in practice; if those terms were added,
# an explicit exclusion against emergencies/HIV/TB_malaria/NTD would be needed.
```

### Sexual health and wellbeing

> This category captures recommendations addressing sexual health and wellbeing as defined by
> WHO... The category also includes recommendations on infertility and on the recognition of
> sexual rights more broadly.
>
> Decision rule: This category does not include recommendations specifically addressing
> maternal health, abortion, family planning, contraception, or comprehensive sexuality
> education, which are classified under their respective categories.

```{r}
#| label: kw-sexual-health
sexual_health_keywords <- "reproductive health|reproductive rights|reproductive medicine|reproductive human rights|reproductive services|access to fertility services|sexual health|reproductive and mental health|reproductive, maternal|reproductive and maternal|reproductive life skills|sexual, reproductive and child health services|sexual and reproductive and health|reproductive and family planning services|reproductive information and services| srhr|regulate assisted reproductive methods"

sexual_health_keywords_comb1a <- "sexual relation|sexual conduct|same-sex|gay|lesbian|same sex|homosexual|sexual activit"
sexual_health_keywords_comb1b <- "criminal"

sexual_health_keywords_comb2a <- "sex|gender|civil identity|transgender"
sexual_health_keywords_comb2b <- "sterili"
```

#### Other SRHR

Broad sexual/reproductive-health terms used to populate the *SRHR (combined)* parent and a
residual *Other SRHR* tag for SRHR content not captured by a specific SRHR sub-theme.

```{r}
#| label: kw-srhr
srhr_keywords <- "reproductive|sex education|sexuality education|education on sexual|breastfe|access to fertility services|sexual health| sexual education"

srhr_keywords_comb1a <- "sex|gender|civil identity|transgender"
srhr_keywords_comb1b <- "sterili"

srhr_keywords_comb2a <- "sexual relation|sexual conduct|same-sex|gay|lesbian|same sex|homosexual"
srhr_keywords_comb2b <- "criminal"
```

### Sexual education

> This category captures recommendations addressing comprehensive sexuality education...

```{r}
#| label: kw-sexual-education
sexual_education_keywords <- "sex education|sexuality education|education on sexual|education in sexuality|sexual education|reproductive information|human rights education, in particular on sexual and reproductive rights and health|educational programmes on sexual and reproductive health|reproductive health education"

sexual_education_keywords_comb1a <- "reproductive health|sexual health"
sexual_education_keywords_comb1b <- "education"
```

### Mental health

> This category covers recommendations addressing mental health... Recommendations addressing
> mental disabilities, suicide and suicide prevention, and psychosocial development or support
> services are included in this category.

```{r}
#| label: kw-mental
mental_keywords <- "anxiety|depression|suicide|psychosocial|psychiatr|involuntary hospital|forced hospital|mental health"

mental_keywords_comb1a <- " mental|^mental"
mental_keywords_comb1b <- "health|disorder|illness|treatment"
```

### Gender-based violence and harmful practices

> This category covers recommendations addressing violence perpetrated against individuals on
> the basis of sex, gender identity or expression, or sexual orientation, as well as harmful
> traditional practices...
>
> Cross-classification rules: Recommendations on violence, rape, or forced sex work involving
> children are dual-classified under Child and adolescent health... Forced abortion is
> dual-classified with Abortion...

```{r}
#| label: kw-gbv
GBV_keywords <- "child marriage|early marriage|forced marriage|marriage of children|minimum age of marriage|minimum age for marriage|marriage for girls and boys under the age |age of marriage|mutilation|fgm|excision|honor crime|honor killing|honour crime|honour killing|breast ironing|harmful practices|harmful traditional practices|violence against women|domestic violence|family violence|violence in the family|gender-based violence|sexual violence|marital violence|partner violence|gender violence| rape|^rape|virginity|sexual abuse|femicide|polygam|sex selection|prenatal selection|prenatal genetic|dowry|bride kidnap|child bride|forced steril|coerced steril|istanbul convention| conversion therap|sexual harassment|levirate|sexual exploit|sexually exploit|sex traffic|sexual and labour exploitation|child prostitution|forced prostitution|invasive pregnancy test|domestic violence|domestic abuse|domestic maltreatment|forced anal exam"

GBV_keywords_comb1a <- " forced|minimum|under the age of"
GBV_keywords_comb1b <- "marriage|pregnancy"

GBV_keywords_comb2a <- "girl|women|sexual|gender|marital|lgbt|lgtb|same-sex|intersex|sexual minorit"
GBV_keywords_comb2b <- "abuse|maltreatment|violence"

GBV_keywords_comb3a <- "abort"
GBV_keywords_comb3b <- "select| forced|coercive"

GBV_keywords_comb4a <- "lgbt|lgtb| gay|lesbian|transgender|intersex|same sex|same-sex|bisexual"
GBV_keywords_comb4b <- "surg|medical"
GBV_keywords_comb4c <- "unnecessary| forced|consent|non-consensual|irreversible"

GBV_keywords_comb5a <- "child|infant|adolescent| teen| boy| girl| crc| minors "
GBV_keywords_comb5b <- "victims of abuse|corporal punishment|violen|prostitut|sexual abuse|sexual exploitation| rape|^rape"

GBV_keywords_comb6a <- "sex"
GBV_keywords_comb6b <- "traffic"
```

### Women's health

> This category captures recommendations addressing women's health from a general health-system
> perspective rather than through the lenses of violence, sexual and reproductive health and
> rights, maternal health, family planning, abortion, sexual health, or infertility...

```{r}
#| label: kw-women
women_keywords <- "menstrua|breast cancer|cervical cancer"

women_keywords_comb1a <- "woman|women|cedaw"
women_keywords_comb1b <- "mortality|health"

# REFINEMENT (women's-health precision/recall):
# (1) high-precision women's-health phrases that should always tag the theme
women_explicit_keywords <- "menstrua|breast cancer|cervical cancer|women's health|woman's health|health of women|women's right to health|women's access to health|gynaecolog|gynecolog|health care for women|health-care for women|healthcare for women|health services for women|reproductive cancer"
# (2) the catch-all now requires a woman term to co-occur with a HEALTH/MEDICAL-services term
#     (not merely any health-related flag), so women's-rights/socioeconomic recs are not swept in
women_term_keywords    <- "women|woman"
women_health_services  <- "health|medic|hospital|clinic|medicine|gynaecolog|gynecolog| disease|mortalit| hiv |cancer|mental health|immunis|immuniz|vaccin"
# (3) recs that enumerate >=3 vulnerable-group categories are general/non-discrimination recs in
#     which "women" is incidental; these are excluded from the catch-all (see cross-classification)
women_group_patterns <- c(
  women    = "women|woman|girl",
  child    = "child|adolescent|infant| boy| minor",
  disab    = "disabilit|disabled",
  migrant  = "migrant|refugee|asylum|immigrant|displaced",
  elderly  = "older person|older people|elderly|the aged|senior citizen",
  minority = "minorit|indigenous|ethnic|afro-",
  lgbt     = "lgbt|lesbian| gay|transgender|intersex"
)
```

### Child and adolescent health

> This category covers recommendations addressing fetal, newborn, infant, child, and adolescent
> health...
>
> Decision rule: Recommendations on early or child marriage are not classified here; they fall
> under Gender-based violence and harmful practices. Recommendations on corporal punishment are
> classified under this category... only when they are clearly situated in the context of
> children, schools, the home, or "all settings".

```{r}
#| label: kw-cah
CAH_keywords <- "newborn| infant|neonatal|new-born|breastfe|protection of women and children|bacha bazi|violence against children|childhood cancer|early pregnanc|killing of children|maiming of children|child sex tourism"

CAH_keywords_comb1a <- "child|infant|adolescent| teen| boy| girl| crc| minors "
CAH_keywords_comb1b <- "victims of abuse|death|mortality|pregnan|corporal punishment|flogging|amputation|health|violen|prostitut|sexual abuse|sexual exploitation|ill-treatment|cancer| rape|with hiv| hiv |hiv/aids|hiv-aids|hiv and aids|hiv & aids|hiv-positive| hiv,| hiv-|prevention of aids|fight against aids|combating aids| sti | std | stds | stis |sti,| std,| stds,| stis,|chlamydia|gonorrhea|syphilis|herpes|papillomavirus| hpv | hpv,|trichomoniasis|sexually transm|sexual transm|antiretroviral|anti-retroviral"
NOT_CAH_keywords_comb1c <- "death penalty|death sentence|capital punishment"

# REFINEMENT (FN): the codebook classifies corporal punishment under CAH (dual GBV) when it is
# situated in the context of children, schools, the home, or "all settings" - even when no child
# word is present (e.g. "ban corporal punishment in schools"). Context terms for that rule:
corporal_punishment_context <- "school|at home|in the home|in homes|in the family|within the family|family setting|all settings|all places|all aspects of society|alternative care|care setting|care institution|day nurser|day care| institution|upbringing|child-rearing"
```

### Maternal health

> This category covers recommendations addressing maternal health, including pregnancy,
> childbirth, and the postpartum period...
>
> Decision rule: Recommendations focused exclusively on preventing or reducing early or teenage
> pregnancies - without addressing care for active pregnancies - are not classified under
> Maternal health and are instead classified under Family planning and contraception.
> Recommendations related to abortion are classified only under Abortion.

```{r}
#| label: kw-maternal
maternal_keywords <- "maternity services|maternity health|maternity care|maternity protection|maternal|midwif|midwiv|antenatal|prenatal|pre-natal|pre natal|postnatal|birth atten|stillbirth|eclampsia|obstetric|miscarr|intrapartum|postpartum|post-partum|breastfe|safe motherhood|protecting maternity|health of the mother|life of the mother|mothers' health|mother and child health|mothers and newborns to full health care|care for children and mothers|health of the pregnant woman|health of pregnant women|life of the pregnant woman|mother's life|mother's health|life of the mother|health of the mother|child pregnan|early pregnan|adolescent pregnan|(?=.*pregnant teen)(?=.*health|mortality|medical)|teenage pregnan|safe pregnan|health during pregnancy|post natal|post-natal|pregnant women with free access to quality health|adolescent mother|pregnant person is a child|treatment programmes for pregnant women|health of mother|health of the mother|mother's health|mothers' health|maternity, disease and health|medical care during all their stages of life, including during pregnancy|therapeutic abortion|safe abortion|infected mother|(?=.*childbirth)(?=.*health|mortality|medical)|(?=.*mother)(?=.*health-care|healthcare|health care|mortality|medical)"

maternal_keywords_comb1a <- "motherhood"
maternal_keywords_comb1b <- "health"

maternal_keywords_comb2a <- "pregnant wom|pregnant mother|pregnant adolescent|pregnant teen"
maternal_keywords_comb2b <- "health care|healthcare|medical care|health services|health coverage|(?=.*medical)(?=.*assistance)"
maternal_keywords_comb2c <- " free|access|guarantee|increase|provid"

NOT_maternal_keywords <- "miscarriage of justice|miscarriages of justice|maternal transfer"

# When a recommendation is also an Abortion recommendation, it is only counted as Maternal
# health if it falls within one of these maternal/abortion-risk contexts (matched against the
# ORIGINAL-case text, exactly as in the first attempt):
maternal_abortion_context <- "unsafe abortion|risk|health of the pregnant woman|health of pregnant women|life of the pregnant woman|mother's life|mother's health|life of the mother|health of the mother|incompatible with extrauterine life|physical integrity of women|maternal mortality|health of the woman|life of the woman|woman's health|woman's life|women's rights to life|women's rights to health|women's right to both life and health|women's right to health|health for all women|life is endangered as a result of pregnancy|physical integrity of women|women's universal right to life|woman's or a girl's life|rape, incest and life endangerment|rape, life or health endangerment|life-saving abortion|in cases of health hazards|woman is in danger|health is in dander|health is endangered|health is seriously endangered|life is endangered|save their life|severe fetal impairment|threats to the pregnant woman|well-being of the pregnant|threat to health|health hazards to the mother|maternal protection|obstetric emergen|miscarr|therapeutic abortion"
```

### Abortion

> This category covers recommendations addressing abortion...
>
> Cross-classification rules: Recommendations addressing forced or coercive abortions are
> dual-classified with Gender-based violence and harmful practices.

```{r}
#| label: kw-abortion
abortion_keywords <- "abortion|foetal impairment|fetal impairment"

abortion_keywords_comb1a <- "interrup|terminat"
abortion_keywords_comb1b <- "pregnan"
```

### Family planning and contraception

> This category covers recommendations addressing family planning and contraception... This
> category also includes recommendations using language such as "reducing teenage pregnancies"
> or "bringing down the rate of early pregnancies", where the focus is on prevention rather than
> care during an active pregnancy.

```{r}
#| label: kw-contraception
contraception_keywords <- "contracept|birth control| iud |condom|family planning|regulation of fertility|eradication of pregnancies|pregnancy test"

contraception_keywords_comb1a <- "uninten|unwanted|child|teen|early|adolescent|girl| minors "
contraception_keywords_comb1b <- "pregn"
contraception_keywords_comb1c <- "avoid|prevent|protect|reduc|prevalence"
```

### Disabilities and health

> This category covers recommendations addressing the health and access to health services of
> persons with disabilities...
>
> Decision rule: Recommendations not explicitly addressing health issues... are not included
> under this category.

```{r}
#| label: kw-disabilities
disabilities_keywords_comb1a <- "health|hospital|clinic|medical|psychiat|doctor|nurs|disorder|illness|diabet|disease|care institution|care service"
disabilities_keywords_comb1b <- "disabil|disabled|crpd|handicap"
```

### Health of LGBTQI persons

> This category covers recommendations addressing the health and access to health services of
> lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, and intersex (LGBTQI) persons...

```{r}
#| label: kw-lgbti
LGBTI_keywords <- "hormone therapy|requirement of infertility|requirement of sterilisation|requirement of sterilization|requirement for infertility|requirement for sterilisation|requirement for sterilization|need for sterilization|mandatory sterility|coerced into sterilization| unwanted gender reassignment|gender affirmation treatment"

LGBTI_keywords_comb1a <- "lgbt|lgtb| gay|lesbian|transgender|intersex|same sex|same-sex|bisexual| trans |transsexual|trans-specific|sexual minorit"
LGBTI_keywords_comb1b <- "health|gender reassignment|medical"

NOT_LGBTI_keywords <- "trans mountain|implement the trans health strategy|trans pacific"
```

### HIV/AIDS and STIs

> This category covers recommendations addressing HIV/AIDS and other sexually transmitted
> infections (STIs)...

```{r}
#| label: kw-hiv
HIV_keywords <- "with hiv| hiv |hiv/aids|hiv-aids|hiv and aids|hiv & aids|hiv-positive| hiv,| hiv-|prevention of aids|fight against aids|combating aids|aids pandemic| sti | std | stds | stis |sti,| std,| stds,| stis,|chlamydia|gonorrhea|syphilis|herpes|papillomavirus| hpv | hpv,|trichomoniasis|sexually transm|sexual transm|antiretroviral|anti-retroviral"
```

### Tuberculosis, malaria, and neglected tropical diseases

> This category covers recommendations addressing tuberculosis, malaria, and neglected tropical
> diseases...

```{r}
#| label: kw-tb-ntd
TB_malaria_keywords <- "tuberculosis|malaria| tb | tb,"

NTD_keywords <- "buruli|chagas|dengue|chikungunya|dracunculiasis|echinococcosis|trematod|trypano|sleeping sickness|leishmaniasis|leprosy|leper|filariasis|mycetoma|chromoblastomycosis| noma |onchocerciasis|rabies|scabies|schistosomiasis|helminth|snakebite|snake|taenia|trachoma|yaws"
```

### Vaccines, pharmaceuticals, and health products

> (Codebook folds these into Health systems and services.) Kept here as a standalone sub-theme
> that **also tags its parent, Health systems and services**.

```{r}
#| label: kw-vaccines
vaccinations_keywords <- "vaccin|immunisation|immunization|medicine|antibiotic|antimicrobial|essential medicine"

essential_medicines_keywords <- "medicine|antibiotic|antimicrobial"
```

### Water and sanitation

> This category covers recommendations addressing access to safe drinking water and basic
> sanitation services...

```{r}
#| label: kw-wash
WASH_keywords <- "sanitation|safe water|drinking water|drinkable water|affordable water|clean water|access to water|toilet|cholera|water and safe environment"
```

### Nutrition

> This category covers recommendations addressing nutrition and diet-related health concerns...

```{r}
#| label: kw-nutrition
nutrition_keywords <- "nutrition|nutritious|diet|obesity|nutrient|food insecurit|food securit"
```

### Health of incarcerated persons

> This category covers recommendations addressing the health of incarcerated persons...
>
> Decision rule: Recommendations are classified here only when they specifically address
> health-related dimensions. (Applied here by requiring the recommendation to be health-related.)

```{r}
#| label: kw-incarcerated
incarcerated_keywords <- " prison|imprison|detention|detaine|incarcer"
```

### Occupational health

> This category covers recommendations addressing the promotion and protection of physical,
> mental, and social well-being among workers across all occupations...

```{r}
#| label: kw-occupational
occupational_health_keywords <- "health of workers|workers' health|occupational health|safe and healthy work environment|safe work environment|healthy work environment|occupational cancer"
```

### Refugee and migrant health

> This category covers recommendations addressing the health of refugees, asylum seekers, and
> migrants.

```{r}
#| label: kw-migrant
migrant_health_keywords_comb1a <- "migrant|refugee|asylum|undocumented|illegal alien"
migrant_health_keywords_comb1b <- "health"
```

## The theme registry

Each theme that can be matched directly from text is assembled into a `spec`. Themes that
depend on *other* themes (Women's health, Maternal health, Health of incarcerated persons, the
Refugee/migrant expansion, the parent aggregates, and the Health-related umbrella) are handled
afterwards in the cross-classification layer.

```{r}
#| label: registry
specs <- list(
  
  health_systems = spec(
    rule(hss_keywords),
    rule(essential_medicines_keywords),                       # essential medicines -> health systems
    rule(vaccinations_keywords),                              # vaccines/immunization -> health systems
    rule(hss_system_keywords),                                # FN refinement: system-/population-level
    rule(c(hss_keywords_comb1a, hss_keywords_comb1b)),
    rule(c(hss_keywords_comb2a, hss_keywords_comb2b, hss_keywords_comb2c))
  ),
  
  emergencies = spec(
    rule(emergencies_keywords),
    rule(c(emergencies_keywords_comb1a, emergencies_keywords_comb1b))
    # Note: comb2 (disaster relief) defined in the codebook keywords but, as in the first
    # attempt, not wired into the emergencies spec. Left as-is for comparability.
  ),
  
  ncd = spec(
    rule(ncd_keywords),
    rule(c(ncd_keywords_comb1a, ncd_keywords_comb1b)),
    rule(c(ncd_keywords_comb2a, ncd_keywords_comb2b))
  ),
  
  communicable = spec(
    rule(communicable_keywords),
    rule(communicable_keywords_comb1a, none = communicable_keywords_comb1b)  # virus, but not HIV
  ),
  
  SRHR = spec(                                                 # residual "Other SRHR"
    rule(srhr_keywords),
    rule(c(srhr_keywords_comb1a, srhr_keywords_comb1b)),
    rule(c(srhr_keywords_comb2a, srhr_keywords_comb2b))
  ),
  
  sexual_health = spec(
    rule(sexual_health_keywords),
    rule(c(sexual_health_keywords_comb1a, sexual_health_keywords_comb1b)),
    rule(c(sexual_health_keywords_comb2a, sexual_health_keywords_comb2b))
  ),
  
  sexual_education = spec(
    rule(sexual_education_keywords),
    rule(c(sexual_education_keywords_comb1a, sexual_education_keywords_comb1b))
  ),
  
  mental_health = spec(
    rule(mental_keywords),
    rule(c(mental_keywords_comb1a, mental_keywords_comb1b))
  ),
  
  GBV = spec(
    rule(GBV_keywords),
    rule(c(GBV_keywords_comb1a, GBV_keywords_comb1b)),
    rule(c(GBV_keywords_comb2a, GBV_keywords_comb2b)),
    rule(c(GBV_keywords_comb3a, GBV_keywords_comb3b)),
    rule(c(GBV_keywords_comb4a, GBV_keywords_comb4b, GBV_keywords_comb4c)),
    rule(c(GBV_keywords_comb5a, GBV_keywords_comb5b)),
    rule(c(GBV_keywords_comb6a, GBV_keywords_comb6b)),
    rule(c("corporal punishment", corporal_punishment_context))   # FN refinement (see kw-cah)
  ),

  CAH = spec(
    rule(CAH_keywords),
    rule(c(CAH_keywords_comb1a, CAH_keywords_comb1b), none = NOT_CAH_keywords_comb1c),
    rule(c("corporal punishment", corporal_punishment_context))   # FN refinement (see kw-cah)
  ),
  
  disabilities = spec(
    rule(c(disabilities_keywords_comb1a, disabilities_keywords_comb1b))
  ),
  
  LGBTI = spec(
    rule(LGBTI_keywords),
    rule(c(LGBTI_keywords_comb1a, LGBTI_keywords_comb1b)),
    exclude = NOT_LGBTI_keywords
  ),
  
  HIV = spec(
    rule(HIV_keywords)
  ),
  
  TB_malaria = spec(
    rule(TB_malaria_keywords)
  ),
  
  NTD = spec(
    rule(NTD_keywords)
  ),
  
  vaccinations = spec(
    rule(vaccinations_keywords)
  ),
  
  WASH = spec(
    rule(WASH_keywords)
  ),
  
  nutrition = spec(
    rule(nutrition_keywords)
  ),
  
  abortion = spec(
    rule(abortion_keywords),
    rule(c(abortion_keywords_comb1a, abortion_keywords_comb1b))
  ),
  
  contraception = spec(
    rule(contraception_keywords),
    rule(c(contraception_keywords_comb1a, contraception_keywords_comb1b, contraception_keywords_comb1c))
  ),
  
  occupational_health = spec(
    rule(occupational_health_keywords)
  )
)
```

### QA keyword patterns

For transparency, a combined positive-keyword pattern per theme (including the themes derived in
the cross-classification layer). Used only by the spot-check tables to show which terms matched.

```{r}
#| label: qa-patterns
collapse_pat <- function(...) paste(c(...), collapse = "|")

qa_patterns <- list(
  health_systems       = collapse_pat(hss_keywords, hss_keywords_comb1b, vaccinations_keywords, hss_other_keywords),
  vaccinations         = vaccinations_keywords,
  emergencies          = collapse_pat(emergencies_keywords, emergencies_keywords_comb1a),
  ncd                  = collapse_pat(ncd_keywords, ncd_keywords_comb1a, ncd_keywords_comb2a),
  communicable         = collapse_pat(communicable_keywords, communicable_keywords_comb1a),
  HIV                  = HIV_keywords,
  TB_malaria           = TB_malaria_keywords,
  NTD                  = NTD_keywords,
  TB_malaria_NTD       = collapse_pat(TB_malaria_keywords, NTD_keywords),
  SRHR                 = collapse_pat(srhr_keywords, srhr_keywords_comb1a),
  sexual_health        = sexual_health_keywords,
  sexual_education     = sexual_education_keywords,
  maternal_health      = collapse_pat(maternal_keywords, maternal_keywords_comb1a, maternal_keywords_comb2a),
  abortion             = collapse_pat(abortion_keywords, abortion_keywords_comb1a),
  contraception        = collapse_pat(contraception_keywords, contraception_keywords_comb1a),
  mental_health        = collapse_pat(mental_keywords, mental_keywords_comb1a),
  GBV                  = collapse_pat(GBV_keywords, GBV_keywords_comb2a),
  women                = collapse_pat(women_keywords, women_keywords_comb1a),
  CAH                  = collapse_pat(CAH_keywords, CAH_keywords_comb1a),
  disabilities         = disabilities_keywords_comb1b,
  LGBTI                = collapse_pat(LGBTI_keywords, LGBTI_keywords_comb1a),
  WASH                 = WASH_keywords,
  nutrition            = nutrition_keywords,
  incarcerated         = incarcerated_keywords,
  occupational_health  = occupational_health_keywords,
  migrant_health       = migrant_health_keywords_comb1a
)
```

## Apply the registry

```{r}
#| label: apply-registry
# Run every spec over the (lower-cased) recommendation text -> a logical flag per theme.
flags <- as_tibble(lapply(specs, function(s) match_spec(txt, s)))
flags$rowid <- uhri_upr$rowid
```

## Cross-classification layer

The codebook's decision rules and theme inter-dependencies are applied here, in an explicit and
documented order. This reproduces the staged logic of the first attempt.

```{r}
#| label: cross-classification

# Derived aggregate: TB, malaria, and NTDs ------------------------------------------------
flags$TB_malaria_NTD <- flags$TB_malaria | flags$NTD

# REFINEMENT (FP, codebook): "Recommendations related to HIV, STIs, tuberculosis and malaria and
# other Neglected Tropical Diseases are excluded from [Communicable diseases]". Drop the overlap
# so HIV/TB/NTD recs are not double-counted in the generic communicable bucket (they remain under
# their specific categories, and still roll up to Communicable diseases (combined)).
flags$communicable <- flags$communicable & !(flags$HIV | flags$TB_malaria | flags$NTD)

# Shared "health context" vocabulary used to gate themes that the codebook only counts when an
# explicit health dimension is present (incarcerated persons; refugees/migrants). Deliberately
# excludes bare "treatment" (would match "ill-treatment") and bare "mental" (would match
# "fundamental").
# NOTE: deliberately EXCLUDES bare "disabilit" (would tag anti-discrimination "vulnerable groups
# such as ... persons with disabilities" lists that have no health dimension) and bare "treatment"
# (would match "ill-treatment"). Includes maternal/pregnancy-care terms so e.g. "prenatal care for
# pregnant women prisoners" is recognised as a health dimension.
health_context_keywords <- "health|medic|hospital|clinic|sanitation|sanitary|hygiene|nutrition| disease|illness|psychiatr|mental health|psychosocial|vaccin|medicine|doctor| nurse|drinking water|reproductive health| hiv |epidem|pregnan|prenatal|postnatal|antenatal|breastfe|maternal"

# Stage 1 of the Health-related umbrella --------------------------------------------------
# Health-related if it matched ANY of the directly-detected themes so far, OR if it contains a
# broad health key-term and is not on the NOT_health list.
stage1_themes <- c("health_systems","emergencies","ncd","communicable","SRHR",
                   "sexual_health","sexual_education","mental_health","GBV","CAH",
                   "disabilities","LGBTI","HIV","TB_malaria","NTD","TB_malaria_NTD",
                   "vaccinations","WASH","nutrition")
any_stage1 <- reduce(flags[stage1_themes], `|`)

flags$health_related <- any_stage1 |
  (!str_detect(txt, NOT_health_keywords) & str_detect(txt, health_keywords))

# Health of incarcerated persons --------------------------------------------------------------
# REFINEMENT (FP, codebook): "classified here only when they specifically address health-related
# dimensions (e.g. physical health, mental health, sanitation, nutrition). Recommendations
# addressing torture or corporal punishment in detention are not classified under this category."
# The previous rule (health_related & prison-term) wrongly tagged rape/torture/arbitrary-detention
# recs that are health_related via GBV. Require an explicit health-context term instead.
flags$incarcerated <- str_detect(txt, incarcerated_keywords) &
  str_detect(txt, health_context_keywords)

# Refugee and migrant health ------------------------------------------------------------------
# REFINEMENT (FP, codebook): the category is "the health of refugees, asylum seekers, and
# migrants" - i.e. it requires a health dimension. The previous expansion (migrant-term &
# health_related) tagged GBV recs that merely mention migrants ("violence against women,
# including migrant workers"). Require a migrant term AND an explicit health-context term.
flags$migrant_health <- str_detect(txt, migrant_health_keywords_comb1a) &
  str_detect(txt, health_context_keywords)

# Maternal health (depends on Abortion) ---------------------------------------------------
# - excluded outright by NOT_maternal_keywords
# - if also an Abortion rec: only Maternal when an at-risk maternal/abortion context is present
#   (matched against ORIGINAL-case text, as in the first attempt)
# - otherwise the standard maternal keyword/combination logic
flags$maternal_health <- dplyr::case_when(
  str_detect(txt, NOT_maternal_keywords) ~ FALSE,
  flags$abortion & str_detect(txt_raw, maternal_abortion_context) ~ TRUE,
  flags$abortion ~ FALSE,
  str_detect(txt, maternal_keywords) ~ TRUE,
  str_detect(txt, maternal_keywords_comb1a) & str_detect(txt, maternal_keywords_comb1b) ~ TRUE,
  str_detect(txt, maternal_keywords_comb2a) &
    str_detect(txt, maternal_keywords_comb2b) &
    str_detect(txt, maternal_keywords_comb2c) ~ TRUE,
  .default = FALSE
)

# REFINEMENT (FP, codebook): "Recommendations focused exclusively on preventing or reducing early
# or teenage pregnancies - without addressing care for active pregnancies - are not classified
# under Maternal health and are instead classified under Family planning and contraception."
# Remove the Maternal tag when the only maternal signal is early/teen/child-pregnancy framed as
# prevention, and no active maternal-care term is present (these recs already match contraception).
maternal_pregnancy_terms <- "teenage pregnan|teen pregnan|early pregnan|adolescent pregnan|child pregnan|child and adolescent pregnan|unplanned.*pregnan|adolescent.*pregnan"
maternal_prevention_lang <- "prevent|reduc|avoid|combat|lower|rate of|prevalence|incidence|tackl|address the high|bring down|curb|fight against"
maternal_care_terms <- "antenatal|prenatal|pre-natal|post-natal|postnatal|postpartum|post-partum|obstetric|maternal mortalit|maternal and child mortalit|maternity|midwif|midwiv|childbirth|skilled birth|birth atten|eclampsia|miscarr|safe motherhood|motherhood|health of the mother|life of the mother|mother's health|mother's life|during pregnancy|pregnant wom|pregnant mother|maternal health|maternal care|perinatal|neonatal|care for those affected|care during"
maternal_prevention_only <- str_detect(txt, maternal_pregnancy_terms) &
  str_detect(txt, maternal_prevention_lang) &
  !str_detect(txt, maternal_care_terms)
flags$maternal_health <- flags$maternal_health & !maternal_prevention_only

# Stage 2 of the umbrella: add themes detected after stage 1 -------------------------------
flags$health_related <- flags$health_related |
  flags$abortion | flags$maternal_health | flags$contraception |
  flags$occupational_health | flags$migrant_health

# Health systems "data" top-up (only when already health-related) -------------------------
flags$health_systems <- flags$health_systems |
  (flags$health_related & str_detect(txt, hss_other_keywords))

# Women's health (general, gendered health; excludes GBV/SRHR/maternal/FP/abortion) --------
# REFINEMENT: the old catch-all (health_related & "women") swept in women's-rights/socioeconomic
# recs and vulnerable-group enumerations. It now (a) always tags high-precision women's-health
# phrases, (b) keeps CEDAW ratification, (c) drops recs that enumerate >=3 vulnerable-group
# categories, and (d) otherwise requires a woman term AND a health/medical-services term.
women_group_count <- Reduce(`+`, lapply(women_group_patterns, function(p) as.integer(str_detect(txt, p))))
women_enumeration <- women_group_count >= 3
# NOTE: this codebook's Women's-health definition (menstrual health, cervical cancer, women's
# access to general health services) does NOT include bare CEDAW ratification, so - unlike the
# first attempt - a stand-alone "ratify CEDAW" recommendation is not tagged here. CEDAW recs that
# also concern women's health are still caught by the women + health-services branch below.
flags$women <- dplyr::case_when(
  # Exclusion first: the codebook defines women's health as a gendered lens on GENERAL health,
  # explicitly NOT through the violence / SRHR / maternal / FP / abortion lenses.
  flags$GBV | flags$SRHR | flags$maternal_health | flags$contraception | flags$abortion ~ FALSE,
  str_detect(txt, women_explicit_keywords) ~ TRUE,
  women_enumeration ~ FALSE,
  str_detect(txt, women_term_keywords) & str_detect(txt, women_health_services) ~ TRUE,
  .default = FALSE
)

# Stage 3 of the umbrella: add Women's health ---------------------------------------------
flags$health_related <- flags$health_related | flags$women
# (migrant_health and incarcerated are now gated by an explicit health-context term above, so
#  they no longer depend on - nor expand - the Health-related umbrella.)
```

## Hierarchy and parent propagation

> If a recommendation falls into a sub-theme it should also be tagged into its parent theme.

```{r}
#| label: hierarchy
# Parent aggregates: a parent is TRUE whenever any of its children is TRUE.
flags$SRHR_any <- reduce(
  flags[c("SRHR","sexual_health","sexual_education","maternal_health","abortion","contraception")],
  `|`
)
flags$Communicable_combined <- reduce(
  flags[c("communicable","HIV","TB_malaria_NTD")],
  `|`
)
# (TB_malaria_NTD already aggregates TB_malaria + NTD; vaccinations already feeds health_systems
#  via the registry; health_related already aggregates every theme via the staged logic above.)

# Catch-all: health-related but not matched by any specific (leaf) theme -------------------
leaf_themes <- c("health_systems","vaccinations","emergencies","ncd",
                 "communicable","HIV","TB_malaria","NTD",
                 "SRHR","sexual_health","sexual_education","maternal_health","abortion","contraception",
                 "mental_health","GBV","women","CAH","disabilities","LGBTI",
                 "WASH","nutrition","incarcerated","occupational_health","migrant_health")
any_leaf <- reduce(flags[leaf_themes], `|`)

# Safety net: guarantee the "tag the parent" invariant - every leaf theme implies Health-related.
flags$health_related <- flags$health_related | any_leaf

flags$other_health_related <- flags$health_related & !any_leaf

# REFINEMENT (FN): rescue still-residual recommendations that express general health-system /
# population-health / medical-access language into Health systems and services. Applied ONLY to the
# residual, so recs already in a specific theme can never be over-tagged by an incidental mention.
hss_rescue <- flags$other_health_related & str_detect(txt, hss_residual_access)
flags$health_systems       <- flags$health_systems | hss_rescue
flags$other_health_related <- flags$other_health_related & !hss_rescue
```

## Assemble the outputs

### Theme metadata

A single table describing every output theme: its variable name, display label (from
`theme_labels.R`), parent, and level in the hierarchy.

```{r}
#| label: theme-meta
theme_meta <- tribble(
  ~name,                  ~parent,                  ~level,
  "health_related",        NA,                       "umbrella",
  "health_systems",        "health_related",         "theme",
  "vaccinations",          "health_systems",         "subtheme",
  "emergencies",           "health_related",         "theme",
  "ncd",                   "health_related",         "theme",
  "Communicable_combined", "health_related",         "aggregate",
  "communicable",          "Communicable_combined",  "subtheme",
  "HIV",                   "Communicable_combined",  "subtheme",
  "TB_malaria_NTD",        "Communicable_combined",  "aggregate",
  "TB_malaria",            "TB_malaria_NTD",         "subtheme",
  "NTD",                   "TB_malaria_NTD",         "subtheme",
  "SRHR_any",              "health_related",         "aggregate",
  "SRHR",                  "SRHR_any",               "subtheme",
  "sexual_health",         "SRHR_any",               "subtheme",
  "sexual_education",      "SRHR_any",               "subtheme",
  "maternal_health",       "SRHR_any",               "subtheme",
  "abortion",              "SRHR_any",               "subtheme",
  "contraception",         "SRHR_any",               "subtheme",
  "mental_health",         "health_related",         "theme",
  "GBV",                   "health_related",         "theme",
  "women",                 "health_related",         "theme",
  "CAH",                   "health_related",         "theme",
  "disabilities",          "health_related",         "theme",
  "LGBTI",                 "health_related",         "theme",
  "WASH",                  "health_related",         "theme",
  "nutrition",             "health_related",         "theme",
  "incarcerated",          "health_related",         "theme",
  "occupational_health",   "health_related",         "theme",
  "migrant_health",        "health_related",         "theme",
  "other_health_related",  "health_related",         "theme"
) |>
  left_join(theme_labels, by = c("name" = "variable")) |>
  mutate(label = coalesce(theme_label, name)) |>
  select(name, label, parent, level)

# Order used for the wide output columns.
theme_order <- theme_meta$name
```

### Wide table (one column per theme)

Downstream-compatible: every theme is a factor with levels `c("Other", <label>)`, mirroring the
first attempt's encoding, with the cleaned text relocated to the front.

```{r}
#| label: wide
to_factor <- function(flag, label) factor(if_else(flag, label, "Other"),
                                          levels = c("Other", label))

classified <- uhri_upr
for (nm in theme_order) {
  classified[[nm]] <- to_factor(flags[[nm]], theme_meta$label[theme_meta$name == nm])
}

classified <- classified |>
  relocate(all_of(theme_order), .after = text)
```

### Tidy long table (one row per recommendation × theme)

Convenient for multi-label analysis. Includes the parent/level so the leaf themes, parent
aggregates, and the umbrella can be filtered apart.

```{r}
#| label: long
classified_long <- flags |>
  select(rowid, all_of(theme_order)) |>
  pivot_longer(-rowid, names_to = "name", values_to = "tagged") |>
  filter(tagged) |>
  left_join(theme_meta, by = "name") |>
  select(rowid, theme = name, label, parent, level) |>
  arrange(rowid)
```

### Save

```{r}
#| label: save
saveRDS(classified,      here("output", "UHRI_UPR_classified.rds"))
# saveRDS(classified,      here("output", paste0("UHRI_UPR_classified_", Sys.Date(), ".rds")))
saveRDS(classified_long, here("output", "UHRI_UPR_classified_long.rds"))
```

## Quality assurance

### Counts per theme

```{r}
#| label: qa-counts
theme_counts <- flags |>
  select(all_of(theme_order)) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), sum)) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "name", values_to = "n") |>
  left_join(theme_meta, by = "name") |>
  mutate(pct = round(100 * n / nrow(flags), 1)) |>
  select(level, label, parent, n, pct)

theme_counts |>
  arrange(match(label, theme_meta$label)) |>
  DT::datatable(
    rownames = FALSE,
    options = list(pageLength = 35, dom = "t"),
    caption = "Number of recommendations tagged per theme (out of the full dataset)."
  )
```

### How many themes per recommendation?

```{r}
#| label: qa-multilabel
# Count leaf-theme tags only (exclude umbrella + aggregates), to see the multi-label spread.
tags_per_rec <- rowSums(as.matrix(flags[leaf_themes]))
tibble(n_themes = tags_per_rec) |>
  count(n_themes, name = "n_recommendations") |>
  mutate(pct = round(100 * n_recommendations / sum(n_recommendations), 1)) |>
  DT::datatable(rownames = FALSE, options = list(dom = "t"),
                caption = "Distribution of the number of (leaf) themes assigned per recommendation.")
```

### Spot-check a theme with matched keywords

Set `qa_theme` to any leaf theme name to review a sample of tagged recommendations together with
the keywords that triggered the match. This is the main transparency aid: every tag is traceable
to the literal terms it matched.

> **Reading a blank `matched_keywords`.** This column is populated from the theme's *direct*
> keyword set (`qa_patterns`). A blank value therefore means the recommendation was tagged by a
> **cross-classification rule rather than a direct keyword match** — i.e. no direct keyword
> matched, but a decision rule in the cross-classification layer assigned the theme. The clearest
> example is `maternal_health`: an *Abortion* recommendation occurring in a maternal/abortion-risk
> context (matched on the raw text via `maternal_abortion_context`, e.g. "risk", "woman's life")
> is routed into Maternal health, yet contains none of the maternal keywords, so this column is
> blank. The blank is the honest signal here, since no direct keyword did in fact match.

```{r}
#| label: qa-spotcheck
qa_theme <- "maternal_health"   # <- change to inspect a different theme

# Identify the tagged rows from the already-computed `classified` object first, then run the
# (relatively expensive) keyword extraction only on that subset rather than the whole dataset.
# NOTE: `matched_keywords` is drawn from the theme's DIRECT keyword set only. A blank value
# means the row was tagged by a cross-classification rule, not a direct keyword (see the note
# above the chunk) - e.g. abortion-in-a-risk-context recommendations routed into maternal_health.
qa_rows <- which(classified[[qa_theme]] != "Other")

tibble(
  rowid              = classified$rowid[qa_rows],
  state_under_review = classified$state_under_review[qa_rows],
  matched_keywords   = matched_terms(txt[qa_rows], qa_patterns[[qa_theme]]),
  text_2             = classified$text_2[qa_rows]
) |>
  DT::datatable(
    filter = "top",
    options = list(pageLength = 25, scrollX = TRUE),
    rownames = FALSE,
    class = "cell-border stripe hover compact",
    caption = paste0("Recommendations tagged '", qa_theme, "' with the keywords that matched.")
  )
```

### Health-related vs. not

```{r}
#| label: qa-health-related
flags |>
  count(health_related, name = "n") |>
  mutate(pct = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) |>
  DT::datatable(rownames = FALSE, options = list(dom = "t"),
                caption = "Overall split: health-related vs. not.")
```

## Notes and refinements

### Accuracy refinements applied (vs. the first attempt)

Each is marked in the code with a `# REFINEMENT` comment and was validated by inspecting the
recommendations that changed:

- **Communicable diseases — exclude HIV / TB / malaria / NTD overlap** (codebook). HIV/TB/NTD recs
  no longer double-count in the generic communicable bucket (they remain under their specific
  categories and still roll up to *Communicable diseases (combined)*).
- **NCD — tighten the drug-use combination.** The bare `" use "` token matched "use of the death
  penalty / force"; it is replaced with genuine drug-use/consumption language, removing
  death-penalty-for-drug-offences false positives (and incidentally catching "drug users").
- **CAH & GBV — corporal-punishment context gating** (codebook FN). Corporal punishment in
  schools / the home / "all settings" is now tagged (and dual-classified) even without an explicit
  child word.
- **Maternal health — prevention-vs-care routing** (codebook). Recommendations focused purely on
  *preventing/reducing* early or teenage pregnancy (no active-care term) are no longer tagged
  Maternal; they remain under Family planning.
- **Incarcerated & migrant/refugee health — require an explicit health dimension** (codebook).
  These no longer rely on the broad Health-related flag (which let GBV/torture/discrimination recs
  in); they now require a health-context term (`health_context_keywords`).
- **Right-to-health umbrella — drop violence-context "blood".** "bloodshed" / "bloody repression"
  are excluded via `NOT_health_keywords`.
- **Women's health — replace the broad catch-all.** The old `health_related & "women"` rule swept in
  women's-rights/socioeconomic recs and vulnerable-group enumerations. It now: tags high-precision
  women's-health phrases (after the SRHR/violence exclusion), drops recs that enumerate ≥3
  vulnerable-group categories, and otherwise requires a woman term **and** a health/medical-services
  term. (Note: this codebook does not list CEDAW ratification under women's health, so stand-alone
  "ratify CEDAW" recs are no longer tagged here.)
- **Health systems — recover general system/population-health recs.** System-level language
  (`hss_system_keywords`: health sector/system/financing/policy/workforce, UHC, right to health,
  "health of the population", primary-care reform…) now tags Health systems on any rec
  (dual-classifying alongside a specific theme, per the codebook), and a residual-only sweep
  (`hss_residual_access`) rescues general "access to medical care / national health policy" recs
  from *Other health-related*.

### Still open (candidates for a future pass)

- **Sexual trafficking/exploitation of women & girls → GBV.** "trafficking in women/girls" without the word "sex" is not yet captured by the GBV trafficking rule.
- **`women`** remains the most judgment-dependent category by nature (the line between women's
  *health* and women's *rights/socioeconomic* recs is genuinely blurry); the current rule is much
  tighter but a handful of borderline calls will always exist.
- **`disabilities` / `occupational_health`** rely on their keyword sets alone (no separate
  health-context gate); they currently look clean but were not tightened.
```
